name: FluidType
description: Stream fluid type

name: GlobalComponents
description: Global GlobalComponents option to display component fractions or flows on molar or mass basis

name: Components
description: Stream Components option to display component fractions or flows on molar or mass basis.\nSet Components to Global and change it globally using GlobalComponents.

name: Phase
description: Stream selection to display phase properties for multiphase streams

name: Thermophysical
description: Stream selection to calculate thermophysical properties (Cp, mu, kappa, sigma)

name: CpCvRatio
description: Stream selection to calculate ideal specific heat ratio (CpCv) or real isentropic expansion coefficient (k)

name: Critical
description: Stream selection to calculate critical and reduced temperature and pressure

name: Fuel
description: Stream selection to calculate fuel properties for a compositional stream (GHV, LHV, Wobbe Index and Soot Index)

name: DewBubblePoint
description:
Stream selection to calculate the degree of superheating and subcooling for multiphase fluids
     Dew: this option reports
         - the stream dew point temperature Td at the actual pressure.
         - the stream delta temperature DTd above the dew point (T-Td) at the actual pressure.
     Bubble: this option reports
         - the stream bubble point temperature Tb at the actual pressure.
         - the delta temperature DTb above the bubble point (T-Tb) at the actual pressure.
         - the stream vapor pressure Pv at the actual temperature.
     Both: this option combines the options for Dew and Bubble. 
     Hide: dew and bubble point data are not reported.
The variables Td, DTd, Tb, DTb, Pv are listed in the “Dew and Bubble Point” group.

name: StandardProperties
description:
Stream StandardProperties option to calculate standard liquid and vapor volumetric flowrates, L and V
     - L is based on pure components specific gravity, SG60F, which is at 60 F and 1 atm
     - V is based on ideal gas conversion of moles to volume
           - Nm3 are reported at 0 C and 1 atm
           - scf are reported at 60 F and 1 atm
   Hide: Do not calculate
   Dry: Calculate on dry basis (excluding water composition) 
   Wet: Calculate on wet basis 
   Both: Calculate both on dry and wet basis

name: CalcBulkLiquid
description: Stream selection to calculate bulk liquid properties for VLLE

name: Resistance
description:
Stream option to enable simplified flow resistance calculations for use in Fluid Flow and Dynamics modes (these modes rely 
on pressure gradients in the simulation to calculate flow rates).
Set Resistance = Flow to provide a simple flow-based linear pressure drop calculation in the stream. If data is available, use
a Pipe or other physical model to rigorously calculate the pressure drop and pressure gradient instead.

name: PropModelType
description: Stream model type to calculate additional stream properties

name: FRramp
description:
Global flow parameter to ramp from forward stream
conditions to reverse stream conditions.

For steady state simulations, use a value smaller than
the smallest flowrate in your simulation.

For dynamic simulations, the FRramp will cause the
thermodynamic state calculations to use the forward
enthalpy/composition when the flow is greater than
FRramp, reverse enthalpy/composition when flow is
less than negative FRramp, and ramp the values in
between negative FRramp and FRramp as flow reversal
takes place.

A larger value will smooth out sudden discontinuities
for dynamics but stream properties may appear wrong
near zero flow in steady state simulations.

name: Show
description: Stream parameter to show Cp for ideal Cp/Cv calculation

name: Elevations
description:
Global selection to enable the specification of equipment port elevations.
     Disabled: port elevations cannot be specified.  Elevation change (DE) can still be specified in the Pipe and PipeRig models.
     Manual: port elevations must be manually specified. DE in the Pipe and PipeRig models can be swapped for port elevation specifications.
     Automatic: port elevations are automatically calculated using equipment geometry and configuration options for select models (Column, Drum, etc).

When automatic elevations are enabled, an elevation specification must be removed from each inlet stream of impacted models (for example, remove the DE specification from an inlet Pipe).
Other model elevations must still be manually specified. For an explanation of how port elevations are estimated, see each model's help file.

name: DPlin
description: Stream DP linearization

name: Phases
description:
Stream option to override the fluid phase selection
     Fluid: The FluidType's phases are applied.
     Vapor: The phases are set to Vapor.
     Liquid: The phases are set to Liquid.
     VLE: The phases are set to VLE.
     VLWE: The phases are set to VLWE.
     VLLE: The phases are set to VLLE.

name: PhaseNoOverride
description: Stream PhaseNoOverride parameter defaulted to NoOverride which is used for the connection to the State.PhaseOverride when Phases = Fluid

name: FlashCheck
description: Stream option to compare stream temperature with upstream flash temperature
